data.title: Northern Star to buy Australian peer De Grey Mining in A$5B deal
data.description:
Northern Star Resources (OTCPK:NESRF) said early Monday it agreed to acquire Australian gold mining peer De Grey Mining (OTCPK:DGMLF) in an all-stock deal valued at ~A$5B (~US$3.26B).

The key asset in the deal De Grey's (OTCPK:DGMLF) Hemi gold development, a Tier 1, low-cost asset in Western Australia's Pilbara region, which is expected to ramp up toward the end of the decade.

Hemi is set to produce 530K oz/year on average in the first 10 years of production, making it one of Australia's top five gold mines.

The project is still subject to a final investment decision, pending government environmental approvals, but Northern Star (OTCPK:NESRF) said it hopes to see first production by mid-2026.

Under the deal terms, De Grey (OTCPK:DGMLF) shareholders will receive 0.119 new Northern Star (OTCPK:NESRF) shares for each share held, implying an offer price of A$2.08/share, a 36.8% premium on De Grey's Friday share close of A$1.52/share.

Northern Star (OTCPK:NESRF) said its shareholders would own ~80% of the combined group, and De Grey (OTCPK:DGMLF) shareholders would hold the rest.

"Northern Star's assets are all aging, becoming more capital-intensive and facing declining grades," Citi analyst Kate McCutcheon said. "While the timing may come as a surprise to the market given recent comments around M&A... we think in the fullness of time, Northern Star can demonstrate its exploration pedigree to unlock value."

data.title: SA Asks: Which retail stocks will win or lose this holiday season?
data.description:
The 2024 holiday shopping season appears to be off to a promising start, with Mastercard (MA) reporting a 3.4% increase in Black Friday sales over last year.

E-commerce saw the biggest burst of activity, with online retail sales jumping 14.6% year-over-year, while sales at brick-and-mortar stores increased only 0.7%, according to Mastercard.

Which brings us to today’s SA Asks question: which retail stocks will win or lose this holiday season?

We asked Seeking Alpha analysts Justin Purohit, Amrita Roy and Julian Lin for their picks.

Justin Purohit: The tale of two performances of Walmart (NYSE:WMT) and Target (NYSE:TGT) highlight a key theme likely to persist this holiday season: despite relief from lower gas prices and easing inflation, consumers remain focused on value, favoring discount retailers while exercising caution on big-ticket discretionary spending.

I believe this dynamic bodes well for discount-focused retailers such as Walmart (WMT) and The TJX Companies (NYSE:TJX). Ollie’s Bargain Outlet (NASDAQ:OLLI), which has already posted a 25% year-to-date gain, is another under-the-radar name with potential for continued growth. I am particularly bullish on Dollar General (NYSE:DG) and Dollar Tree (NASDAQ:DLTR), as overly bearish sentiment toward these two dollar store names sets the stage for a potential upside surprise this holiday season.

Amrita Roy: I am personally very bullish on Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN). Having exceeded both revenue and earnings estimates in its Q3 FY24 earnings, along with a steady trend of upward revenue and earnings per share revisions by analysts, I believe the stock is trading at an attractive valuation.

From a business standpoint, Amazon’s (AMZN) Stores business (Amazon.com, Physical Store), which contributed more than 40% to total revenue in Q3, should continue to benefit from the holiday season as the company prioritizes broad selection, low prices, fast delivery and a range of Prime member benefits to boost sales volume.

The second company that I am bullish on is Lululemon (NASDAQ:LULU). While the company has lagged the S&P 500 year-to-date as a result of sluggish performance in its Americas market, its international markets have seen strong momentum, accounting for a growing share of total revenue. During the holidays season, I expect Lululemon’s (LULU) international markets to continue outperforming as the company opens new stores while simultaneously driving customer engagement through its membership program.

In the meantime, I expect Lululemon’s (LULU) Americas market is likely at a point of inflection, as management doubled down on revamping “newness” in the women’s category, which should see signs of recovery, especially during the holiday season.

Julian Lin: My surprising pick may be Meta Platforms (NASDAQ:META), which has seen its Meta Rayban smart glasses surge in popularity. While this still makes up an insignificant portion of the business, it is promising to see the company's endless investment in its Reality Labs division finally begin to pay off.

Top Consumer Discretionary Stocks

data.title: Stellantis CEO Carlos Tavares resigns amid sales slump
data.description:
Update 5:05 PM ET: Changes Headline, Adds Details

Stellantis (NYSE:STLA) chief executive Carlos Tavares has resigned, the carmaker behind the Peugeot, Jeep and Fiat brands said on Sunday, as it battles a profit slump and weakened U.S. sales.

In a sudden exit for one of the industry's most high-profile executives, Tavares will hand over the reins to a newly created interim committee headed by chairman John Elkann. The company will name a new CEO in the first half of 2025.

Tavares will leave his position earlier than expected after differences in views emerged between him and the board, said independent director Henri de Castries. He was previously expected to step down in early 2026 at the end of his contract.

The veteran automotive executive has been under pressure since Stellantis (STLA) slashed its profit forecast in September and forecast a cash burn of up to 10 billion euros. The automaker mostly blamed slowing sales and bloated inventories in its crucial North American market.

Shares of Stellantis (STLA), the world's fourth largest automaker by sales, have tumbled over 43% so far this year.

Tavares led Stellantis (STLA) since its formation in 2021, through the merger of Fiat Chrysler and Peugeot owner PSA.

The automaker reaffirmed its financial guidance for the year.

Mounting Troubles

According to a Financial Times report, there were increasing tensions between Tavares and Stellantis' (STLA) board on the measures needed to put the company back on track.

"He was focusing on the short term rather than the group’s longer term and managed to anger everybody in the process," said one person familiar with conversations, the report added.

The company's third quarter revenue plunged 27% to 33 billion euros, with all regions except South America posting double-digit declines in revenue. North America led the pack, with third quarter sales plunging 42% to 12.4 billion euros.

Tavares has also faced mounting criticism from its U.S. dealers and the United Auto Workers union over its poor financial performance.

In October, Italian lawmakers grilled Tavares over the company's production plans.

On Thursday, the company said it would halt production at its Italian Mirafiori plant due to continuing poor sales of the electric Fiat 500 city car made there. Stellantis (STLA) also announced plans to shut its van factory in Luton, UK.

data.title: AstraZeneca cancer drug Enhertu added for reimbursement in China despite probe
data.description:
AstraZeneca's (NASDAQ:AZN) blockbuster cancer medication, Enhertu, developed with Japanese pharma giant Daiichi Sankyo (OTCPK:DSKYF) (OTCPK:DSNKY), would be added for reimbursement by state-run medical insurance in China, the country's health officials announced Thursday.

Enhertu's addition to China's National Reimbursement Drug List takes effect on Jan. 1 despite an ongoing probe into the conduct of a top AstraZeneca (NASDAQ:AZN) executive in the country.

The 91-drug list includes anti-inflammatory, anti-microbial, and oncology drugs from the likes of Johnson & Johnson (JNJ), Merck (MRK), Bristol Myers (BMY), Roche (OTCQX:RHHBY) (OTCQX:RHHBF), and Sanofi (SNY).

China's NRDL is a critical regulatory component determining product availability for pharmaceutical companies in the world's second-biggest pharmaceutical market. The drugs selected for this year's list are expected to fall by an average of 63%.

China is AstraZeneca's (AZN) third-biggest market, adding as much as $5.8B in revenue for the company in 2023.

However, the Anglo-Swedish pharma giant's future in the country came under the spotlight last month when Chinese authorities launched an investigation into Leon Wang,President of its China business.

data.title: Novartis raises mid-term sales guidance
data.description:
Ahead of an investor event on Thursday, Swiss drugmaker Novartis (OTCPK:NVSEF) increased its mid-term guidance, indicating a sales CAGR of more than 6% for the 2023–2028 period compared to its prior guidance of over 5%.

The company said it continues to project over 40% of its core operating income margin by 2027, given its strong sales growth and efficiency gains.

“Longer term, we’ve identified more than 30 assets in the pipeline with significant potential to rejuvenate our portfolio and support mid-single-digit growth post 2029,” CEO Vas Narasimhan added.

“Taken together, we remain confident in our focused strategy and ability to deliver sustainable value creation for shareholders.”

However, Novartis (NYSE:NVS) reaffirmed its 2024-2029 sales growth projection at more than 4% of CAGR and indicated a delayed timeline to launch pelabresib, a cancer therapy it added with its recent $3B acquisition of German drugmaker MorphoSys.

At the time of the MorphoSys deal, the company expected to file a regulatory filing as early as this year for the drug targeted at a blood cancer called myelofibrosis.

According to Narasimhan, the pelabresib launch could be delayed until at least 2027, depending on safety data.

"It will take us at least until 2027. It might take longer, depending on the safety topic. And of course, there is the possibility that the safety topic would not allow us to progress the medicine at all," Reuters quoted Narasimhan as saying in a media call.

data.title: Alibaba folds its Tmall and Taobao businesses into a new reporting group
data.description:
Alibaba Group Holding Limited (NYSE:BABA) announced on Thursday the establishment of Alibaba E-commerce Business Group.

The Hangzhou-based company said the businesses that will be integrated into the new group will include Taobao and Tmall Group, Alibaba International Digital Commerce Group, the 1688 Marketplace, Idle Fish and others, covering domestic and international supply chains. Mr. Fan Jiang has been appointed as the chief executive officer of this business group.

Q3 earnings recap: Alibaba (BABA) reported 5% year-on-year revenue growth to $33.70 billion, surpassing analyst expectations of $33.47 billion. However, adjusted earnings per American Depositary Share ere $2.15, below the anticipated $2.26, with adjusted net income declining by 9% to $5.20 billion. The Taobao and Tmall Group's revenue rose by 1% to $14.11 billion, while the International Digital Commerce Group saw a significant 29% increase to $4.51 billion, driven by cross-border operations.

Notably, Alibaba's Cloud Intelligence Group experienced a 7% revenue growth, largely due to advancements in public cloud products. Despite these gains, free cash flow declined by 70% year-over-year, which the company attributed to investments in cloud infrastructure and merchant refunds.

Shares of Alibaba (BABA) traded flat at $86.77 in the premarket session on Thursday. The e-commerce stock is up 12% on a year-to-date basis.

data.title: Terex appoints CFO
data.description:
• Terex (NYSE:TEX) said on Thursday that Jennifer Kong-Picarello will succeed Julie Beck as senior vice president and CFO.
  • Kong-Picarello is also set to become a member of the executive leadership team.
  • TEX shares were trading +0.69% Thursday pre-market at $52.50.
  • She will join the aerial work platforms and materials processing machinery company on Feb. 3, 2025, and assume her duties as CFO later in February after the company files its annual report on Form 10-K.
  • Beck will continue at the company until April 1 to assist with the transition.
  • Kong-Picarello most recently served as senior vice president, CFO, of Energy Management, a €28B segment with six global divisions within Schneider Electric.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Europe natural gas price rises to YTD high on heightened geopolitical risks
data.description:
European natural gas prices reached their highest level of the year on Thursday, raised by strong demand and rising geopolitical risks, particularly Russia's increased attacks against Ukraine's energy infrastructure and fears that escalating tensions in the region could disrupt gas flows.

Investors also are watching Russian pipeline flows to Europe after Kremlin-controlled Gazprom stopped supplies to Austrian gas importer OMV (OTCPK:OMVJF).

Despite the dispute, Russia's natural gas exports through Ukraine to Europe have remained stable; Gazprom said it would send 42.4M cm of gas to Europe via Ukraine on Thursday, the same volume it has shipped each day since November 12.

The benchmark Dutch TTF contract recently traded +1.9% to €47.69/MWh, and is up nearly 20% so far in November.

Meanwhile, European Union gas storage is currently 89.9% full, below the five-year average of 91% for this time of year.

"With storage not as high as initially expected going into the winter and a number of supply risks, speculators continue to favor gas from the long side," ING analysts wrote.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)

data.title: Agrify looks to raise $26M via private placement; shares tumble
data.description:
• Agrify (NASDAQ:AGFY) said it is looking to raise ~$26M through a non-brokered private placement priced at $22.30 per share.
  • The firm expects to use the proceeds from the private placement for general corporate purposes, including, among other things, working capital and business development.
  • The investors in the placement will get pre-funded warrants to the extent any investor’s beneficial ownership of Agrify stock following the offering would exceed 4.99%.
  • Shares slumped 32.4% in premarket trade.
